articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
articles.description: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

images.name: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
images.caption: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

articles.headline: Messi: It's a joy to continue at Inter Miami
articles.description: Lionel Messi says he's "very happy" to continue at Inter Miami after signing a new deal with the club.

images.name: Messi: It's a joy to continue at Inter Miami
images.caption: Lionel Messi says he's "very happy" to continue at Inter Miami after signing a new deal with the club.

articles.headline: Carlo Ancelotti speaks about life as Brazil's head coach
articles.description: Carlo Ancelotti is "having a great time" living in Brazil and managing the national team ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Carlo Ancelotti speaks about life as Brazil's head coach
images.caption: Carlo Ancelotti is "having a great time" living in Brazil and managing the national team ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

images.caption: Raphinha speaks about a transfer offer from Saudi Arabia before Hansi Flick took over Barcelona in 2024.

article.headline: Argentina held by Venezuela on rain-soaked pitch as Messi returns
article.description: Argentina were held to a 1-1 draw against Venezuela on Thursday in Lionel Messi's return to international duty in the South America's World Cup qualifiers.
article.linkText: Argentina held by Venezuela on rain-soaked pitch

images.name: Venezuela vs. Argentina rain [1296x729]
images.alt: Enzo FernÃ¡ndez of Argentina and Jefferson Savarino of Venezuela battle for the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026 South American Qualifier match between Venezuela and Argentina at Estadio Monumental de Maturin on October 10, 2024 in Maturin, Venezuela.

images.name: Did a water-logged pitch hamper Messi and Argentina?
images.caption: The Futbol Americas crew of Seb Salazar, Kasey Keller and Herc Gomez discuss how the water-logged pitch actually played to Venezuela's advantage in their 1-1 draw with Argentina.

article.story:
Argentina were held to a 1-1 draw against Venezuela on Thursday in Lionel Messi's return to international duty in the South America's World Cup qualifiers.

Captain Messi, 37, fully recovered from an injury sustained in the Copa America final against Colombia in July, was brilliant in setting up Otamendi on a drenched pitch to open the scoring 13 minutes after kick-off, which was delayed by half an hour due to heavy rain.

Argentina goalkeeper Gerónimo Rulli, filling in for Emiliano 'Dibu' Martinez, who is serving a two-match ban for offensive behaviour, produced a fine performance to keep out Salomón Rondón's first-half efforts.

However, the determined Venezuelan striker equalised in the second half with an exceptional header from a Yeferson Soteldo's cross to temporarily move his team to sixth in the standings with 11 points.

Argentina sought a winner after boss Lionel Scaloni introduced Leandro Paredes and Lautaro Martínez five minutes from time, but the soggy pitch only increased the World Cup winners' shortcomings.

Argentina remain top of the standings with 19 points and will face Bolivia on Tuesday, while Venezuela visit Paraguay.

keyEvents.text: Goal! Venezuela 0, Argentina 1. Nicolás Otamendi (Argentina) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal following a set piece situation.
José Martínez (Venezuela) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Argentina. Gonzalo Montiel replaces Thiago Almada.
Substitution, Venezuela. Jhonder Cádiz replaces Tomás Rincón.
Substitution, Venezuela. Darwin Machís replaces Jefferson Savarino.
Goal! Venezuela 1, Argentina 1. Salomón Rondón (Venezuela) header from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Yeferson Soteldo with a cross.
Substitution, Argentina. Leonardo Balerdi replaces Giovani Lo Celso.
Substitution, Argentina. Leandro Paredes replaces Enzo Fernández.
Substitution, Argentina. Lautaro Martínez replaces Julián Alvarez.
Substitution, Venezuela. Edson Castillo replaces José Martínez because of an injury.
Germán Pezzella (Argentina) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
